_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to represent and analyse information and data using a range of formats — including complex graphs, maps created with GIS technology, and statistical analysis.

_source.w: We are learning to represent and analyse information and data using a range of formats — including complex graphs, maps created with GIS technology, and statistical analysis.
y: Advanced representation and analysis skills transform raw data into meaningful geographical understanding.
GIS and statistical analysis are professional tools used in urban planning, environmental science, and policy analysis.
These technical skills significantly enhance the quality and credibility of our geographical work.
eg: Creating maps using GIS technology to represent spatial patterns in our data.
Constructing complex graphs — scatter plots, choropleth maps, composite graphs — to analyse geographical data.
Using statistical analysis to identify significant patterns, correlations, or trends.
Selecting the most effective representation format for different types of data and audiences.
Combining multiple representation formats to communicate a comprehensive geographical analysis.
Evaluating the effectiveness of our chosen representations and revising them for clarity.
